headline: Cataract Surgery in KK Nagar, Chennai
subheadline: Make an informed decision about cataract surgery -- timing, lens options, and what to expect -- with AIIMS-trained Dr. Rajeswari.
metaDescription: Stitchless cataract surgery in KK Nagar, Chennai with Dr. Rajeswari. Premium IOL options, painless procedure. Call or book online.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Same Surgeon, Start to Finish
whyPatientsChoose.description: From evaluation through surgery and every follow-up, you're cared for by the same doctor.

symptoms: Glare or halos around lights at night

processSteps.title: Comprehensive Evaluation
processSteps.description: A detailed eye exam to confirm the cataract and assess your eye health.

processSteps.title: Honest Discussion
processSteps.description: We explain your results and IOL options -- no pressure to rush into surgery.

processSteps.title: The Procedure
processSteps.description: Stitchless, painless phacoemulsification, typically with same-day discharge.

landingFaqs.question: How do I know if I really need cataract surgery?
landingFaqs.answer: Surgery is recommended based on how the cataract affects your daily life -- reading, driving, working -- not simply how it appears during examination. We'll discuss what's appropriate for your specific situation.

landingFaqs.question: Can cataract surgery wait, or should it be done immediately?
landingFaqs.answer: In most cases, it can wait until it's genuinely affecting you. There's rarely a need to rush -- some patients prefer to proceed sooner, others are comfortable waiting, and that's a decision we make together.

landingFaqs.question: Will I have time to discuss lens options before making a decision?
landingFaqs.answer: Yes. Lens selection is discussed in detail during your evaluation, with enough time to ask questions before any decision is made.

decisionSupport.suitabilityTitle: When Is Cataract Surgery Usually Recommended?
decisionSupport.suitabilityIntro: Cataract surgery is usually recommended when vision changes begin affecting daily activities such as reading, driving, working, or recognizing faces comfortably. The decision is based on how the cataract affects your life, not simply how it appears during examination. Some patients benefit from surgery earlier, while others may be comfortable waiting -- during your consultation, we'll discuss what is appropriate for your situation.
evaluationSteps: Discussion of Impact on Daily Life

doctorsPerspective: Many patients worry that cataract surgery is being recommended too early, or that they may be choosing the wrong lens. A good cataract consultation should help you understand whether surgery is necessary now, what options are available, and what results you can realistically expect. The goal is not simply to perform surgery -- it's to help you make an informed decision that fits your visual needs and lifestyle.

learnMoreLink.text: Read our full guide to cataract surgery
